description: Mostly sarcastic conversation about meeting culture with one genuine scheduling request buried in the middle. Alice and Bob make fun of meeting overload, then Alice pivots to a real architecture review request for Tuesday at 2 PM.

events.title: Architecture Review
events.reasoning: Alice explicitly asks Bob to 'block Tuesday at two for the architecture review' to go through the new database schema. Bob confirms the time works. Thirty-minute duration specified. The prior sarcastic suggestions (additional standups, Mars all-hands) are clearly humor.

mock_llm_response.summary: One real event extracted: an architecture review on Tuesday Feb 24 at 2:00 PM for 30 minutes. The preceding sarcastic scheduling suggestions are discarded as humor.

notes: Real event buried after sarcastic setup. The model must filter out the sarcastic opening and identify the genuine scheduling request when Alice says 'okay but genuinely' — a clear pivot signal from sarcasm to real intent.
